rationale.R1.2: The delegation is exclusively 'check logical consistency,' making this the load-bearing dimension. The artifact contains at least one critical inferential flaw: the final paragraph conflates classifier AUC (0.93) with calibration as a prevalence estimator, and then erroneously infers that the ~41% contamination rate is validated by the AUC figure—when in fact the 41% figure comes directly from the self-report labels used for training, making that agreement circular rather than confirmatory. Additional consistency issues include (a) claiming deployment requires no self-report labels while the model was trained exclusively on self-reports, and (b) asserting the model is a 'calibrated prevalence estimator' without reporting calibration metrics (ECE, reliability diagram), only discriminative ones (AUC, F1).
rationale.R1.3: Claim-evidence alignment is implicated because the core logical failures manifest as claims that are either unsupported by the reported evidence (calibration claim without calibration metrics) or misread the evidence that is present (AUC cited as support for a prevalence estimate). Assessing consistency requires verifying whether the numbers cited actually license the stated conclusions.
